titleStmt.title: Letter from Michael Collins to Michael Murphy, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael Collins

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Irish Prisoner 48 F
Stafford, Detention Barracks
c/o Chief Postal Censor
London
To Michael Murphy
Irish Prisoner
Lewes. Dear Miceal I have been told that you are
stationed at Lewes and although I haven't seen
your name in the list myself. I'm hoping it's
correct. If this does happen to get you, would
you please give me some particulars about
Jack Hurley who was wounded at Church St.
His mother is very distraught over hearing
nothing at all. G Ward tells me you know.
Was he badly wounded? And was any
provision made on that fateful Saturday
evening for his removal. Kindly also give the
exact place. My brother is going to Dublin
again to make further enquiries.
How are you getting on? & what
do you think of things? You know, I
expect just about what we know & that's
damn little. Your other friends in this
place are as well as can be expected.
No need to tell you keep your heart up.
We'll meet again before the resurrections. Best wishes ,
Michael Collins.
